steps.title: Establish the time frame of the passage
steps.content: The opening clause anchors the passage firmly in a completed historical period: “From 1912 to 1951, Charlotta Bass owned and operated the newspaper.” Both verbs are simple past. The second sentence continues to describe what happened during that same period (“While it was under Bass’s leadership”), so the blank verb must also be past tense to remain consistent.

steps.title: Eliminate non-past forms, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘will become’: Future tense — refers to events that have not yet happened, which contradicts the clearly historical (1912–1951) context. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘became’: Simple past — matches the past-tense verbs “owned” and “operated” and correctly situates the event within the completed historical period. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘is becoming’: Present progressive — implies an ongoing process happening now, which contradicts the historical framing. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘to become’: Infinitive — not a finite verb; it cannot serve as the main verb of the clause. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
